---
title: "Exp 1 — Sanity Checks: Statistical Distance Functions"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
source("../R/distances.R")
dir.create("../results/tables",  recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("../results/figures", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

Verifies that all three distance functions satisfy the sanity checks from the
experiment specification before they are used in clustering (exp2–exp6).

---

## 1  Fisher-Rao Univariate

### Identity and key value

```{r fr_uni_identity}
d_same       <- fisher_rao_univariate(0, 1, 0, 1)
d_0102       <- fisher_rao_univariate(0, 1, 0, 2)
expected_val <- sqrt(2) * log(2)          # ≈ 0.9803  (same-mean special case)

cat("d_F( (0,1), (0,1) ) =", d_same, "       [expected: 0]\n")
cat("d_F( (0,1), (0,2) ) =", round(d_0102, 6),
    "  [expected:", round(expected_val, 6), "]\n")
```

### Symmetry

```{r fr_uni_symmetry}
d_2010 <- fisher_rao_univariate(0, 2, 0, 1)
cat("d_F( (0,1),(0,2) ) == d_F( (0,2),(0,1) ):",
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_0102, d_2010)), "\n")
```

### Distances increase as distributions diverge

```{r fr_uni_monotone}
sigmas <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 50, 100)
dists  <- sapply(sigmas, function(s) fisher_rao_univariate(0, 1, 0, s))

df_inc <- data.frame(sigma2 = sigmas, d_F = round(dists, 6))
knitr::kable(df_inc, caption = "d_F( (0,1), (0, sigma2) ) — should increase with sigma2")

cat("Monotone increasing:", all(diff(dists) > 0), "\n")
```

### sigma → 0 implies d_F → ∞

```{r fr_uni_sigma0}
tiny <- c(1, 0.1, 0.01, 0.001, 0.0001)
dists_small <- sapply(tiny, function(s) fisher_rao_univariate(0, s, 0, 1))

df_small <- data.frame(sigma1 = tiny, d_F = round(dists_small, 3))
knitr::kable(df_small, caption = "d_F( (0, sigma1), (0,1) ) — should grow as sigma1 → 0")

# tiny is decreasing (1 → 0.0001), so distances should be increasing
cat("Grows as sigma → 0:", all(diff(dists_small) > 0), "\n")
```

### Visualisation: distance surface

```{r fr_uni_surface, fig.cap="Fisher-Rao distance d_F((0,1),(mu2,sigma2)) on the parameter plane"}
library(ggplot2)

grid <- expand.grid(
  mu2    = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 60),
  sigma2 = seq(0.2, 4, length.out = 60)
)
grid$d_F <- mapply(fisher_rao_univariate, 0, 1, grid$mu2, grid$sigma2)

p <- ggplot(grid, aes(x = mu2, y = sigma2, fill = d_F)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = expression(d[F]), option = "C") +
  geom_point(aes(x = 0, y = 1), colour = "white", size = 3,
             inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Fisher-Rao distance from (0, 1)",
       x = expression(mu[2]), y = expression(sigma[2])) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p)
ggsave("../results/figures/fr_univariate_surface.png", p,
       width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150)
```

---

## 2  Fisher-Rao Bivariate

```{r fr_biv_checks}
# Identity
d_biv_same <- fisher_rao_bivariate(0, c(1, 2), 0, c(1, 2))

# Symmetry
d_biv_12 <- fisher_rao_bivariate(0, c(1, 1), 0, c(2, 2))
d_biv_21 <- fisher_rao_bivariate(0, c(2, 2), 0, c(1, 1))

# Consistency with univariate: when sigma1 = sigma2 per dim, should equal
# sqrt(2) * sum of squared univariate log-ratios  (same-mean case)
#   d_biv((0,(s,s)),(0,(2s,2s))) = sqrt(2*(ln2)^2 + 2*(ln2)^2) = 2*ln(2)
d_biv_expected <- 2 * log(2)

cat("d_F_biv( same )          =", d_biv_same,
    "        [expected: 0]\n")
cat("d_F_biv( (0,c(1,1)), (0,c(2,2)) ) =", round(d_biv_12, 6),
    "  [expected:", round(d_biv_expected, 6), "]\n")
cat("Symmetric:               ", isTRUE(all.equal(d_biv_12, d_biv_21)), "\n")
```

---

## 3  Hellinger Discrete

```{r hellinger_checks}
p <- c(0.1, 0.4, 0.3, 0.2)
q <- c(0.2, 0.3, 0.3, 0.2)

d_same_h <- hellinger_discrete(p, p)
d_pq     <- hellinger_discrete(p, q)
d_qp     <- hellinger_discrete(q, p)

# Disjoint supports → maximum distance = 1
p_disj <- c(1, 0, 0, 0)
q_disj <- c(0, 1, 0, 0)
d_max  <- hellinger_discrete(p_disj, q_disj)

cat("d_H( p, p ) =", d_same_h, "  [expected: 0]\n")
cat("d_H( p, q ) =", round(d_pq, 6), "\n")
cat("Symmetric:   ", isTRUE(all.equal(d_pq, d_qp)), "\n")
cat("d_H( disjoint supports ) =", d_max, "  [expected: 1]\n")
```

---

## 4  Summary

```{r summary_table, results="asis"}
checks <- data.frame(
  Check = c(
    "FR univariate — d(P,P) = 0",
    "FR univariate — d((0,1),(0,2)) ≈ 0.9803",
    "FR univariate — symmetric",
    "FR univariate — increases with divergence",
    "FR univariate — d → ∞ as sigma → 0",
    "FR bivariate  — d(P,P) = 0",
    "FR bivariate  — d((0,c(1,1)),(0,c(2,2))) ≈ 2*ln2",
    "FR bivariate  — symmetric",
    "Hellinger     — d(p,p) = 0",
    "Hellinger     — symmetric",
    "Hellinger     — max dist = 1 (disjoint support)"
  ),
  Result = c(
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_same, 0)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_0102, expected_val, tolerance = 1e-6)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_0102, d_2010)),
    all(diff(dists) > 0),
    all(diff(dists_small) > 0),    # tiny is decreasing, so distances must increase
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_biv_same, 0)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_biv_12, d_biv_expected, tolerance = 1e-6)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_biv_12, d_biv_21)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_same_h, 0)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_pq, d_qp)),
    isTRUE(all.equal(d_max, 1))
  )
)
checks$Result <- ifelse(checks$Result, "PASS", "FAIL")

knitr::kable(checks, align = c("l", "c"), caption = "Sanity check results")

write.csv(checks, "../results/tables/sanity_checks.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nAll checks passed:", all(checks$Result == "PASS"), "\n")
```
